REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO PROVIDE ANY EVIDENCE IN THE MOVING PAPERS TO CARRY ITS BURDEN TO SHOW A REASONABLE PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. MOVING PARTY'S ATTEMPT TO RECTIFY THIS DEFECT IN THE REPLY IS NOT SUPPORTED BY ANY AUTHORITY. (REPLY 3:25-4:1-2). MOVING PARTY ALSO FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE DEFENDANT DOES NOT HAVE A VALID SECURITY INTEREST IN THE EQUIPMENT. SEE RALEIGH INDUSTRIES OF AMERICA, INC., V. TASSONE (1977) 74 CAL. APP. 3D 692, 698. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-22-2026 Continued To Jul-15-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-25-2026 Continued To Jun-03-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED
Off Calendar Jan-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Howard Klein, Esq. for the Plaintiff Physical Culture Athletic Club LLC via Zoom Video Webinar.
Sebastian Nguyen, Esq. for Defendant 1000 Van Ness LP via Zoom Call.

Hearing held.

Plaintiff argues as to the evidence and provides proof.
Defendant responds to the plaintiff's proof.

After hearing the arguments, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED

is DENIED. Moving party fails to provide any evidence in the moving papers to carry its burden to show a reasonable probability of success on the merits. Moving party's attempt to rectify this defect in the reply is not supported by any authority. (Reply 3:25-4:1-2). Moving party also failed to show that the defendant does not have a valid security interest in the equipment. See Raleigh Industries of America, Inc., v. Tassone (1977) 74 Cal. App. 3d 692, 698.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED
Off Calendar Dec-23-2025 Continued To Jan-08-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED

is continued to January 8, 2026 on the Court's own motion.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED
Off Calendar Dec-15-2025 Continued To Dec-23-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED;

is continued to December 23, 2025. No later than December 16, 2025 opposing party to file a declaration (not to exceed 1 page, excluding caption page) addressing (1) whether a copy of the opposition submitted for filing on December 5, 2025 (subsequently rejected by the clerk of the Court due to the incorrect hearing time listed in the caption) was served on the moving party on December 5, 2025; and (2) whether opposition filed on December 8, 2025 was substantively identical to the opposition submitted for filing on December 5, 2025. Moving party may file a responsive declaration (not to exceed 1 page, excluding caption page), if any, no later than December 18, 2025 addressing solely (1) whether December 5, 2025 copy of the opposition had been served; and (2) if it was served, whether it was substantively identical to the opposition filed on December 8, 2025.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)
